title: The Benefits of Outdoor Activities
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Spending time outdoors and staying (0) physically active is vital for both body and mind. Studies show that regular outdoor exercise leads to (17) significant improvements in cardiovascular health and overall (18) fitness. Activities such as hiking, cycling, and swimming build (19) endurance and strengthen muscles, while also helping people to maintain a (20) healthy weight.||Beyond the physical benefits, spending time in nature has a (21) powerful effect on mental health. Exposure to green spaces has been linked to reduced stress levels and improved (22) concentration. Despite this, many people lead increasingly (23) sedentary lives and fail to get the amount of (24) daily exercise recommended by health professionals, often citing a lack of time or motivation.

example.text: Spending time outdoors and staying (0) PHYSICALLY active is vital for both body and mind. (PHYSICAL)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'significant' is formed from 'signify' by replacing '-ify' with '-icant'.
The noun 'fitness' is formed from 'fit' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
The noun 'endurance' is formed from 'endure' by replacing '-e' with '-ance'.
The adjective 'healthy' is formed from 'health' by adding the suffix '-y'.
The adjective 'powerful' is formed from 'power' by adding the suffix '-ful'.
The noun 'concentration' is formed from 'concentrate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The adjective 'sedentary' describes a lifestyle with little physical activity; it is related to the root 'sedate'.
The adjective/adverb 'daily' is formed from 'day' by replacing '-y' with '-ily'.
